name: Rev
description: Hybrid human + AI transcription and captioning service

developer.comment: American speech-to-text company based in San Francisco and Austin, founded by Jason Chicola, Josh Bardsley, and Paul Lilley.

first-release.comment: Company founded in 2010 and launched its transcription service shortly after; exact month of first public service release not precisely documented, so month omitted. Checked 2026-06-12.

status.comment: Actively developed; VoiceHub platform and ongoing Reverb model releases.

license.comment: The Rev product itself is proprietary SaaS with a free tier (freemium). Note: Rev separately open-sourced its Reverb ASR/diarization models under a non-commercial license, but the consumer tool is freemium.

primary-type.comment: Core purpose is recordings/files to text (human + AI transcription and captioning); also offers an AI Notetaker that joins/records meetings, making it a meeting-assistant.

website.comment: Official product website. Derived from the vendor's own domain.

real-time-dictation.comment: Rev is a transcription/meeting tool, not a speak-to-type dictation app. It offers real-time captioning via API but no productivity dictation that types into your apps.

file-transcription.comment: Core feature: upload audio/video files for AI or human transcription and captioning.

live-meeting-capture.comment: AI Notetaker automatically records and transcribes live meetings from Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Real-time transcription available via WebSocket for live captioning and interactive applications (Rev AI streaming API).

audio-sources.comment: VoiceHub captures from mobile/in-browser recording (microphone), direct file uploads, web URLs, and integrated meeting platforms (Zoom/Meet/Teams) via the AI Notetaker bot.

underlying-engine.comment: Rev's proprietary Reverb ASR model, trained on ~200,000 hours of human-transcribed English audio; benchmarked against and reported to outperform OpenAI Whisper large-v3. Models also released open-source under a non-commercial license.

language-count.comment: Approx 37+ languages on Pro/Unlimited tiers; Free and Essentials plans limited to English (and Spanish). Rev AI async API cites 58+ languages, but the consumer transcription product states 37.

english-accuracy.comment: AI transcription is marketed at 96%+ on clear English; Rev's Reverb engine benchmarks ahead of Whisper large-v3 in independent-style WER tests. Human transcription is 99%+ accurate (near-human). Rated 4 for the AI tool; human service pushes effective accuracy higher.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Supports multiple languages including Spanish and code-switched 'Spanglish'; transcription across 37+ languages.

custom-vocabulary.comment: Custom glossary / Custom Vocabulary lets users add special terms (up to ~500 phrases) to improve recognition; available on subscription tiers and via API.

speaker-diarization.comment: Automatic speaker identification/diarization included; Rev also released Pyannote-based diarization models in Reverb.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: AI Summary automatically generates concise summaries highlighting key points and action items.

built-in-editor.comment: Interactive in-app editor to review and polish transcripts (text correction, not media-by-text editing).

translation.comment: Side-by-side foreign-language translation for transcripts available on Pro and higher tiers (beta).

export-formats.comment: Transcripts export as TXT, DOCX (default), and PDF; captions as SRT and VTT (burned-in captions delivered as MP4 video). No markdown or JSON export documented for the consumer product (JSON is available via the Rev AI API).

processing-location.comment: Cloud-only SaaS; audio is uploaded and processed on Rev's servers. No on-device processing for the consumer product.

offline-capable.comment: Cloud service; requires a network connection to upload and transcribe.

data-used-for-training.comment: Rev does not share customer data with third parties or train external LLMs, but it does train its own proprietary ASR models on customer data to improve speech-to-text; no clearly documented consumer opt-out, so recorded as yes-default. Enterprise/API terms may differ.

data-deletion-control.comment: Users can delete files from their account at any time via the UI; automated deletion policies can be configured; API data is deleted after 30 days.

platforms.comment: Web app plus iOS and Android mobile apps for recording, ordering, and editing. No dedicated desktop (macOS/Windows) app; used via browser.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Not a dictation tool; no OS-wide speak-to-type. Transcription happens within Rev's own app/web interface.

integrations.comment: AI Notetaker integrates with Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams; Dropbox and Zapier connections also available for file routing.

api-automation.comment: Rev AI provides documented async and streaming (WebSocket) speech-to-text APIs that power-users can automate against.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier (freemium) plus paid subscriptions; human transcription and pay-as-you-go AI are usage-based (per-minute: $1.99/min human, $0.25/min AI). Checked 2026-06-12.

free-tier.comment: Genuinely usable free plan with a monthly AI transcription allowance.

free-allowance.comment: Free plan: ~45 minutes/month of AI transcription in English only. Checked 2026-06-12.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid subscription (Essentials) is $25.49/seat/month billed annually (~$29.99 on monthly billing). Human transcription is separately usage-based at $1.99/min. Checked 2026-06-12.
